response.justification: The candidate is a capable and well-rounded full-stack engineer with over 15 years of broad experience, but their expertise is fundamentally misaligned with this role. Their primary stack is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, with no demonstrable depth in Java or Kotlin beyond a brief mention in skills and a peripheral use at Altom for robotics work. There is no Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise JVM framework experience anywhere in the resume, and their only IDE-adjacent work—a 6-month stint contributing C/C++ components to NI LabWindows/CVI—is both dated and unrelated to JVM tooling. This role requires deep JVM internals knowledge and enterprise Java ecosystem expertise as core prerequisites, neither of which the candidate evidences.
strengths: Long track record of building and shipping developer tooling and open-source libraries (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts, Vibe.dav) demonstrating genuine interest in the tooling domain
Prior work contributing to a C IDE (NI LabWindows/CVI) shows at least some historical exposure to IDE development workflows
Strong ownership and polyglot breadth across web, mobile, embedded, and systems domains, suggesting adaptability
concerns: No meaningful Java or Kotlin expertise demonstrated; primary language stack for the past decade has been TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang
Zero visible experience with Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework—the core technical focus of the role
No JVM internals knowledge or IDE tooling experience relevant to Java/Kotlin IDEs; the NI LabWindows work was C-based and over 10 years ago

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework exposure
Developer tooling and open-source library authorship
